title: Jantar Mantar protest LIVE: Delhi High Court agrees to hear PIL seeking probe into Cockroach Janta Party protest
content: CJP spokesperson Ashutosh Ranka reacted to PM Modi’s announcement of fast-track courts, saying that the real issue to be addressed is why paper leaks happen; Delhi Metro shuts at least 16 stations due to the protest

title: Parliament Monsoon Session Day 4 LIVE: LS adjourned for the day amid Oppn. protests; RS to resume proceedings at 3 p.m.
content: Parliamentary Affairs Minister Kiren Rijiju accused the Opposition of evading NEET debate and insisted on government participation; Kharge reiterated demand of Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation

title: In Pictures: CM Vijay's 'Jana Nayagan' opens to fan frenzy
content: Fans across Tamil Nadu celebrated the release of Chief Minister Vijay’s 'Jana Nayagan' with grand FDFS festivities, marking what is being billed as the star-actor’s final film

title: Who is Dharmendra Pradhan? The man at the centre of the CJP education protests
content: Thousands of protesters from the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) took to the streets demanding education reforms and the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan. But who is the man at the centre of the controversy?

title: Man dies days after arrest in Thoothukudi; family alleges custodial torture
content: Arunachalam was arrested for illegal sale of liquor bottles; he was released on July 17, after which his family admitted him to Thoothukudi government hospital, where he was being treated in the neurosurgery ward

title: Why over 250 landless beneficiaries of Kerala’s Arippa land struggle are trapped in bureaucratic deadlock
content: Distribution of title deeds in February 2026 has stalled due to a conflict between Revenue and Forest Departments

title: Social activist Anna Hazare undertakes 'maun andolan' to support protesting students
content: “Anna began his protest at 11 a.m. and ended it at 1 p.m.,” the social activist’s aide Datta Awari told PTI

title: Mask up if you have a heart condition: IMA expert, as Andhra Pradesh COVID-19 cases reach 32
content: Rajeev Jayadevan of the National IMA COVID Task Force said even a fever strains the heart, and that people with heart disease and other conditions should wear masks in crowded places

title: Pedestrian opens latch, detained students escape from police van in Mumbai
content: A video went viral where a man approaches a police van carrying the detained protesters and opens the latch, enabling several students to jump out and run away foiling police attempt to shift student protesters in Mumbai’s Chembur area

title: Kerala actor assault case: HC declines to suspend sentence of two more convicts
content: Division Bench holds that there is sufficient evidence on record to prove the alleged conspiracy and that they had 'executed their plan with precision’

title: Gitanjali Angmo says students’ image being tarnished in media
content: Gitanjali Angmo said those who knew the protesting students had witnessed their extraordinary patience, discipline and restraint

title: FIR against Instagram user for allegedly abusing Prime Minister Narendra Modi over student protest in Delhi
content: The FIR was registered based on a complaint by Abhilash Reddy M (38), president of the BJP unit in Sarvagnanagar constituency

title: Tear gas in India: The law, the science and the human cost
content: As CJP protests reignite debate over ‘Tear Gas’, here’s how tear gas works, the laws governing its use, why it can turn fatal, and what to do if exposed

title: India slams Pakistan at UN over cross-border terrorism; says Kashmir integral, inalienable part
content: At the High-Level Open Debate of the UN Security Council on July 22, India was compelled to reply after Islamabad ‘misused’ the discussion to push a ‘false narrative’ said India's Permanent Representative to the United Nations, Ambassador Parvathaneni Harish

title: Sabarimala gold theft case: SIT arrests former TDB president P.S. Prasanth
content: Former Travancore Devaswom Board member A. Ajikumar too arrested. Both being interrogated at Kollam Police Club

title: You got the date wrong: Jammu and Kashmir CM to Union Minister Nadda on JKSSB paper leak
content: Omar Abdullah was responding to remarks made by J.P. Nadda, where the Union Health Minister accused the National Conference and the Congress of remaining silent on paper leak scandals

title: Delhi HC agrees to hear PIL seeking probe into Cockroach Janta Party protest
content: The Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) has been holding the protest since June 20 to demand the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan over examination irregularities

title: TDB president P.S. Prasanth sees ‘error’ in entrusting Sabarimala artefacts to private person for repair in 2019
content: Prasanth says TDB will transfer the warranty from Unnikrishnan Potty to the board’s name, with High Court’s permission

title: Realtors filed defective PIL on Bidadi township project to derail our protest: Farmers
content: A petition, which was dismissed by the HC, was not only ‘defective’ but also a deliberate attempt by ‘vested interests’ to prevent farmers from pursuing their genuine legal battle against land acquisition

title: Supreme Court may step in if closure of metro stations persists, says CJI
content: Chief Justice of India (CJI) Surya Kant informed the Bar that he had passed an administrative order directing that no adverse orders be passed solely on account of the non-appearance of lawyers

title: School student seriously injured in wild gaur attack in Kerala’s Idukki
content: Boy was waiting for an autorickshaw to take him to school when a wild gaur suddenly emerged and attacked him

title: Bengaluru voices at Freedom Park: ‘Jaldi resign kar Dharmendra Pradhan, Sarjapur jaana hai’
content: Scores of students gathered at Freedom Park in Central Bengaluru condemning the police lathi charge on students who were marching to Parliament protesting against NEET paper leak and irregularities

title: Bengaluru cylinder blast: 10 migrant workers from West Bengal injured in suspected LPG leak explosion at Bommasandra PG
content: The explosion is suspected to have occurred due to an LPG gas leak when the occupants were preparing breakfast.

title: Raja Raghuvanshi murder case: Supreme Court sets aside bail granted to Sonam Raghuvanshi
content: The court directed Sonam Raghuvanshi to surrender within three weeks; the Bench said that Ms. Raghuvanshi’s continued liberty at this stage would “hinder” the ongoing trial

title: The WhatsApp generation made today’s BJP; the Instagram generation is challenging it
content: The only tool that the state has to tame a generation that its parents cannot is brute force. Brute force may succeed in the geographic and social peripheries of the nation, but in the heartland, things are more complicated

title: 10 more deaths raise Assam flood toll to 41
content: Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma says the damage is far greater than anticipated and more bodies are being recovered from the affected districts; more rains forecast over next four days

title: Vilathikulam MLA Markandayan moves Madras High Court seeking to set aside judicial remand order
content: Justice G.K. Ilanthiraiyan grants lunch motion and agrees to hear the matter urgently on Thursday afternoon

title: More delay in NSB formation; Sports Ministry extends deadline for applications
content: The deadline for submission of applications for the selection of its chairman and members has been extended by 15 days, to August 8

title: A. Udhayan appointed Tamil Nadu’s new Head of Forest Force
content: Mr. Udhayan, a 1991-batch IFS officer of the Tamil Nadu cadre, is currently the Director of the Advanced Institute for Wildlife Conservation, Vandalur, a position he has held since June 14, 2022

title: A mountain school and Wangchuk’s experiments with educational reforms
content: Sonam Wangchuk co-founded SECMOL in 1988, and the campus reflects his philosophy: there are no uniforms or rigid classrooms; learning is experiential; and students spend as much time solving practical problems as they do reading textbooks

title: Railway passengers to get online excess baggage booking option from July 31
content: The free and maximum luggage allowances prescribed for AC three-tier, AC chair car and second-class compartments will remain unchanged

title: Mumbai protest: 400 booked, 13 FIRs in five days for protesting without permission
content: A large number of people gathered outside Shiv Sena Bhavan in Dadar and raised slogans against Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan and Prime Minister Narendra Modi

title: Centre considering to lift restrictions on airport ownership
content: The current rules cap airline ownership in airports and are inferred to similarly restrict airport operators from owning airlines

title: India's Ambassador Kwatra meets U.S. Senator Sheehy, discusses defence ties
content: India’s envoy to the U.S. Vinay Mohan Kwatra discussed the vital defence partnership between the two nations and appreciated U.S. Senator Tim Sheehy’s support for the India-U.S. partnership

title: India, U.S. note ‘substantial progress’ on trade deal talks as U.S. Trade Representative Greer ends two-day visit to Delhi
content: U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer held multiple meetings with Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal; however, the two sides have not yet been able to announce a deadline by which the trade deals will be finalised

title: Cockroach Janta Party protests | Collection of The Hindu’s ground reports
content: Tens of thousands of young protesters took to the streets of New Delhi, clashing with police who used tear gas and batons to push them back

title: Banaras-Mumbai Express loco pilot spots iron pipe on tracks, averts accident
content: The RPF has registered a case against unknown persons and launched an investigation into the incident

title: Jantar Mantar protest: Annamalai urges BJP government to engage in meaningful dialogue with students
content: “If our students lose faith in the fairness and credibility of our education system, we are jeopardising the future of our nation,” the former BJP leader said

title: 3,500 VBA workers detained ahead of Maharashtra bandh called by us: Prakash Ambedkar
content: VBA party chief Mr. Ambedkar appealed for a Statewide bandh to be observed in protest of the Centre’s education policies and the alleged police action against students and agitators in Delhi

title: Protesters at Jantar Mantar find relief at free clinics set up by doctors
content: The clinic, apart from having paracetamol, painkillers, bottles of Dettol, and sanitary pads, also has a machine to check blood pressure, an oximeter, and thermometers. The medicines and the equipment were donated by the people and the 10-12 doctors working there

title: VACB’s ‘Operation Parpidam’ exposes widespread municipal corruption in Kerala’s construction sector
content: Statewide inspections also point to how local networks of corrupt officials and their agents have significantly escalated the cost of construction for ordinary families

title: Jana Nayagan gets ‘A’ certificate; likely to release in theatres on July 23
content: According to reports, after the makers implemented modifications suggested by the examining committee, the film was initially considered suitable for a 'U/A' certificate; however, the process stalled when a committee member raised an internal objection, claiming certain portions could hurt public sentiments.

title: Vijayapura MLA Basanagouda Patil Yatnal seeks action against fake news
content: The MLA distributed awards to senior journalists and felicitated meritorious children of journalists. The award ceremony was held at the Kandgal Hanumantharaya Ranga Mandir

title: Allahabad HC split on ‘bulldozer justice’: So what divided the Bench | Explained
content: Two judges differed on how far the High Court can go in creating additional safeguards beyond those already laid down by the Supreme Court

title: Government to set up fast-track courts for paper leak cases, says PM Modi
content: “Nothing is more important than the welfare and future of our youth!” said Prime Minister Narendra Modi

title: No deliverance: when gig workers fall prey to road accidents
content: As the number of workers employed by delivery platforms increases, data from various States, collected by trade unions, show that accidents, injuries, and deaths among them have been on the rise over the years. Though the Union government maintains that gig workers will be covered under the Code of Social Security, details made available in Parliament show that many families of the workers who died or suffered injuries during work did not receive the accidental or death insurance benefits under the Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Yojana (PMSBY)

title: Quality research and ethical publications key to academic success, says CUK Vice-Chancellor
content: CUK V-C Prof. Battu Sathyanarayana urged research scholars to choose meaningful research topics, avoid plagiarism, deposit their theses in Shodhganga, publish in reputed journals, and uphold the highest standards of academic integrity

title: Tobacco farmers meet Jagan Mohan Reddy, seek faster procurement; YSRCP promises to press government
content: Though 94 days have passed since the commencement of tobacco auctions, procurement is far from complete, leaving thousands of growers in severe financial distress, they say

title: Bengaluru Tech Summit ropes in European partners to create global growth opportunities for startups
content: The collaborations are aimed at providing start-ups in Karnataka access to international markets, investors, innovation networks and cross-border partnerships

title: Centre positive on cutting GST on tobacco exports from 40% to 28%: Yashwanth Kumar
content: The Union Government has assured the A.P. delegation that alternative incentive mechanisms will be explored to safeguard farmers’ interests

title: 'Will book you in drug case': Mumbai cop threatens student protesters on cam; inquiry ordered
content: Mumbai Police ordered an inquiry into a viral video showing a police driver threatening students. The driver allegedly warned of fabricated criminal cases and drug planting charges. This incident occurred while Mumbai witnessed protests supporting a New Delhi student agitation. The driver has been removed from his current posting pending the inquiry's completion. Police are verifying facts to address the serious allegations raised by the video.

title: Anna Hazare begins maun andolan in support of protesting students
content: Anna Hazare started a silent protest in Ahilyanagar supporting Delhi students.  Students are protesting alleged examination irregularities and paper leaks.  The Cockroach Janta Party also demands accountability and the minister's resignation.  Clashes occurred between demonstrators and police during a march in Delhi.  Sonam Wangchuk remains hospitalized after a prolonged hunger strike.

title: Woman stands in front of Mumbai Police vehicle during CJP protest, video goes viral
content: A young woman has become a symbol of protests against the alleged NEET paper leak. Rhiya Ahir confronted police in Mumbai, questioning their response to student demonstrations. The Cockroach Janta Party's protests are expanding across cities following earlier clashes in Delhi. Police have registered multiple cases and booked hundreds for unlawful assembly and violating orders. Protesters demand the Union education minister's resignation or direct engagement with students.

title: India's first CWG 2026 medal is already assured before the opening ceremony - Here's how
content: India has secured its first medal at the Commonwealth Games 2026 before the opening ceremony, with Olympic medallist Lovlina Borgohain assured of at least bronze after receiving a semifinal bye. She faces T.K.B.P. Taafaki on July 31 for a place in the final. India begins its Glasgow campaign with a 124-member contingent targeting success across multiple sports.

title: India’s parking crisis is turning into a major civic headache
content: India faces a severe parking shortage due to rapid motorisation and poor urban planning.  Vehicle numbers have doubled in a decade, overwhelming city infrastructure.  Historic areas and newer suburbs struggle with limited space for growing car ownership.  Informal parking thrives while multi-level facilities remain underutilized and enforcement is challenging.  Technology and better public transit are key to managing this growing urban problem.

title: US tells MIT & others: Scientists can help beat China more than universities
content: The US government plans to fund individual scientists directly, moving away from large institutions. This strategic shift aims to accelerate technological discovery and maintain American leadership in science. Federal agencies are tasked with submitting action plans within ninety days for implementation. The report emphasizes revitalizing science and technology through AI and new research organizations. This initiative seeks to ensure scientific advancements benefit all Americans across the nation.

title: UP housing board to demolish 44 illegal properties; traders express concern for livelihood loss
content: UP housing board will demolish 44 sealed shops in Meerut's Shastri Nagar area. This action follows a Supreme Court order regarding illegal constructions and commercial activities. Property owners have fifteen days to comply with demolition notices. Many traders face livelihood crises due to the impending demolition of their businesses. Officials are working to identify and address all non-compliant properties across the city.

title: 10 biggest numbers from Google quarter that has made 'history'
content: Alphabet reported a strong second quarter with $119.8 billion in revenue. Google Cloud saw an impressive 82% year-over-year revenue jump. Gemini app reached 950 million active users, and Search AI Mode crossed one billion. YouTube advertising revenue increased by 13% to over $11 billion. Google's AI models are processing 22 billion tokens every minute.

title: Canada college to protesting Indian students: Seek legal advice, retain imp documents
content: Indian students protest after Portage College's business management diploma graduates faced work visa rejections.  The Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada stated their programs were not eligible for permits.  Portage College encourages affected students to retain important documents and seek legal advice.  The college cannot overturn decisions made by the immigration authority.  Affected students should consult official government materials and obtain independent advice.

title: Bamboo fans, shared shade and pure grit keep Jantar Mantar alive amid CJP protest
content: Volunteers are providing essential support at the CJP protest. They offer shade and water to demonstrators enduring the heat. Many individuals stepped in without formal assignments to help. Young and old alike demonstrate unwavering resolve and solidarity. This organic network ensures the protest continues despite challenges.

title: Orcas filmed ramming giant sunfish until it broke into thousands of pieces
content: Researchers have documented a previously unknown hunting behaviour in orcas in the Gulf of California. The animals were filmed working together to hold and ram giant sharp-tail sunfish until the carcass broke into thousands of pieces. Scientists believe the behaviour may help younger orcas feed more easily or could simply be play.

title: Tony Parker’s 53-acre Texas mansion with private water park lists for $11M
content: Tony Parker's Texas mansion has seen another significant price reduction. The sprawling estate is now listed for eleven million dollars after several adjustments. This luxurious property features an enormous private water park and extensive sports facilities. Despite extensive publicity and a past peak price of twenty million dollars, it remains unsold. The highly customized luxury estate continues its search for a new owner.

title: Mark Cuban to firms using AI agents to cut jobs: Employees know
content: Mark Cuban argues AI agents may overlook human judgment in business decisions.  He believes people understand consequences which AI models currently do not grasp.  Anthropic's report suggests AI has not yet significantly increased unemployment rates.  AI currently augments human expertise rather than fully replacing workers.  Experts note AI's rapid advancement while human oversight remains essential.

title: DDA launches housing scheme with 25% discount: 1,200+ flats, starting from Rs 33 lakh
content: The Delhi Development Authority has launched a new housing scheme for government employees and professionals. This scheme offers over twelve hundred ready-to-move flats with a significant twenty-five percent discount. Registration for these affordable homes opens on July twenty-fourth and booking begins August fifteenth. Flats are located in Narela and will be allotted on a first come, first served basis.

title: 'Hamesha der kar deta hoon...': Tharoor quotes Niazi to target PM's paper leak response
content: Shashi Tharoor used a poetic couplet to criticize the government's paper leak response. Prime Minister Modi announced fast-track courts for swift punishment for offenders. This action plan aims to safeguard the future and welfare of the nation's youth. The Congress party has described the Centre's response as significantly delayed. Protests continue at Jantar Mantar and a hunger strike enters its twenty-sixth day.

title: 'Bite incidents every day': Delhi HC takes up stray dog issue, launches suo motu PIL
content: The Delhi High Court has started a suo motu public interest litigation to monitor stray dog management.  Notices were issued to the Delhi government and Municipal Corporation of Delhi.  Authorities must submit a status report detailing steps taken by July thirty-first.  The court expressed concern over the increasing stray dog population in the city.  This action follows Supreme Court directions for all High Courts to oversee compliance.

title: 'Name is Nothing': CJP on PM Modi's 'nothing more important than youth' post
content: Prime Minister Modi announced fast-track courts for NEET paper leak cases. Abhijeet Dipke of Cockroach Janta Party criticized the government's approach. He satirically highlighted the demand for Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation. Protesters continue demonstrations seeking Pradhan's removal and compensation for affected students. The group insists the minister's resignation remains a non-negotiable demand.

title: Once played by Vallabhbhai Patel, bridge seeks India's 'chess' moment: 17-yo Anshul leads its revival
content: Once enjoyed by Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel, bridge has faded from India's sporting landscape. Seventeen-year-old prodigy Anshul Bhatt hopes to change that. Inspired by Viswanathan Anand's belief that bridge is where chess was two decades ago, Anshul is winning world titles, mentoring young players, conducting research, and building a movement to give the mind sport its long-awaited breakthrough in India.

title: PV Sindhu's China Open campaign ends in heartbreak
content: PV Sindhu's China Open campaign ended in the second round after a spirited 21-16, 20-22, 18-21 defeat to world No. 4 Chen Yufei. Fresh from her Japan Open title, Sindhu squandered four match points in the second game before the Tokyo Olympic champion staged a comeback to win a gripping 90-minute contest and reach the quarterfinals.

title: Simone Biles reveals her biggest fear and how Jonathan Owens helped her
content: Simone Biles has inspired millions with her Olympic success, but her journey was shaped by childhood hardship and a lasting fear of financial insecurity. This story explores how those early experiences influenced her relationship with money, the struggles she overcame before becoming a gymnastics legend, and how NFL star Jonathan Owens became a constant source of love and support. From foster care to Olympic glory, Biles’ story is not just about winning gold medals but also about finding stability, trust, and happiness beyond the sport.

title: Historic relocation of 30 beluga whales begins from closed Canada's Marine park
content: In a significant conservation effort, six beluga whales have been relocated from Canada's Marineland to various aquariums in the United States. This operation, which involved careful planning and the use of specialized transport containers, saw four whales arriving at the Shedd Aquarium while two found a new home at SeaWorld San Antonio.

title: Booked 2 plots, got no agreement: Delhi consumer panel orders builder to refund Rs 19.73 lakh
content: A consumer commission ordered a developer to refund nearly twenty lakh rupees.  This decision followed a housing project delay and the absence of a signed agreement.  The Indian Air Force officer had booked two plots and paid the full amount.  He approached the commission after the developer refused a refund or alternative project.  The commission ruled that without a binding contract, a refund was the appropriate resolution.

title: 'Jana Nayagan' box office collection day 1: Thalapathy Vijay's farewell film opens strong
content: Jana Nayagan opens strong at the box office, eyeing Rs 80 crore worldwide debut. The film collected Rs 11.37 crore India net from morning shows. Tamil Nadu is driving the collections, contributing significantly to the opening day gross. The first weekend is crucial for sustaining momentum into the second week. Strong family audience support could prove a key factor for steady collections.

title: Delivered online from across the country: Food and support reach CJP protest
content: Supporters nationwide send food and medicines to protesters at Jantar Mantar.  Students organized a golgappa cart to serve the assembled demonstrators.  Delivery riders navigate barricades to hand over packages to protest attendees.  Volunteers manage food and medicine distribution points, offering their services.  Students also work to maintain the protest site's cleanliness and peaceful nature.

title: Neeraj Chopra, Mirabai Chanu lead India's medal hopes at CWG 2026
content: India begins its Commonwealth Games campaign in Glasgow with a compact contingent.  The program features ten sports, a significant reduction from previous games.  Athletics forms the backbone of the campaign with many competitors.  Mirabai Chanu and Lovlina Borgohain are key figures for India.  Glasgow offers a learning opportunity before India hosts the games in 2030.

title: 'Pushing through with painkillers': CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke falls ill
content: CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke announced his temporary absence from the Jantar Mantar protest site. He has been suffering from fever and severe body pain for several days. Dipke plans to rest for a few hours and will return to the protest later. The ongoing agitation demands the education minister's resignation over the NEET paper leak. Protesters continue their demonstration despite heightened security and police actions.

title: Rohit IN; Samson OUT: Ex-India cricketer picks India squad for 2027 ODI World Cup
content: Former opener Abhinav Mukund has revealed his 2027 ODI World Cup squad. He included veteran Rohit Sharma and excluded Sanju Samson and Yashasvi Jaiswal. Shubman Gill is now close to the top ODI batting ranking. Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma also feature in the top five batters. Kuldeep Yadav slipped in the bowler rankings after the recent series.

title: R Madhavan reacts to jantar mantar student protest amid backlash, faces online criticism
content: R Madhavan faced backlash for staying silent on the CJP-led protest demanding education minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation over the NEET paper leak. He finally shared a note supporting reforms and urging strict action, but netizens slammed it as a "safe play." The protest, ongoing since June 6, saw Sonam Wangchuk's hunger strike and a Chalo Sansad march.

title: IIT Delhi Roommates Built a ₹565 Crore Startup Transforming India's Cattle Market
content: IIT Delhi civil engineering graduates Neetu Yadav and Kirti Jangra chose an unconventional entrepreneurial path by building Animall Technologies, a digital marketplace for cattle trading. Starting as a weekend project in 2019, the startup aimed to solve challenges faced by dairy farmers buying and selling cows and buffaloes. Today, Animall is valued at around Rs 565 crore and is helping modernise India's largely offline cattle market by connecting farmers through technology and creating a more transparent livestock ecosystem.

title: Tear gas, stones, bottles: Video shows late-night clash during CJP protest in Delhi
content: Fresh violence erupted during a Cockroach Janta Party protest in Delhi, injuring an assistant commissioner of police. Protesters allegedly hurled stones and bottles at security personnel deployed near Tolstoy Marg. CCTV footage from a July 20 protest corroborated police claims of protesters attacking security personnel. The Cockroach Janta Party rejected the police version, accusing them of excessive force against demonstrators.

title: Balakrishna health update: Hospital confirms possible left knee muscle tear
content: Telugu actor Nandamuri Balakrishna sustained a left knee injury while filming.He was initially treated locally before being airlifted for specialized evaluation.Doctors confirmed a localized collection of blood and inflammation around his knee.Production house reassured fans that the injury is minor and recovery is expected.Industry colleagues have sent wishes for his swift and complete recovery.

title: Office cab driver rapes Hyderabad woman on way to airport
content: A cabin crew member was allegedly raped by her cab driver while traveling to the airport. Police registered a rape case and arrested the 23-year-old driver from Shamshabad. The accused confessed to the offense during interrogation by police officials. He had taken a detour and forced himself upon the woman inside the cab. The woman contacted police with help and was sent for medical examination.

title: 'Youth welfare top priority': PM Modi announces fast-track courts for paper leak cases
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi announced fast-track courts for paper leak cases. This move aims to ensure swift and stringent punishment for offenders. Opposition leader Rahul Gandhi criticized the Prime Minister, holding him responsible. Rahul reiterated student demands including the education minister's dismissal. The government vows strict action against those harming students' futures.

title: 'P*** Espanya': Spain's Ferran Torres WC mural vandalised in Barcelona
content: A mural honouring Spain's World Cup hero Ferran Torres was vandalised within 24 hours of being unveiled in Barcelona. Created by street artist TVBoy, it was repeatedly defaced with political graffiti, including "volem seleccions catalanes" ("we want Catalan national teams"), before being removed by city authorities. Despite its brief existence, the artwork highlighted the intersection of football, art and Catalan politics.

title: ‘Dhurandhar’ actress detained during Mumbai student protest; shares video
content: 'Dhurandhar' actress Ayesha Khan revealed she was detained by Mumbai Police on July 22 for standing on the road near the Jantar Mantar-inspired CJP protest. She shared videos from inside the police van, questioning the lack of explanation. Ayesha had earlier urged paparazzi and media to cover the Delhi protest.

title: R&B engineer who took up US job 20 years ago still on govt rolls
content: A Telangana engineer remains on government rolls after two decades abroad. He left for the US in 2006 without mandatory approval and returned briefly. The state government has initiated disciplinary proceedings against the engineer for misconduct. These proceedings aim to determine charges and decide further action for termination. The engineer has not submitted a defense statement to the inquiry authority.

title: Commonwealth Games 2026: India's full schedule, dates and events
content: A contingent of 125 Indian athletes will compete in Glasgow for the 2026 Commonwealth Games. India will participate across thirteen sports disciplines, including several para-sports events. Olympic champion Neeraj Chopra and Mirabai Chanu are expected to lead the medal charge for India. The schedule details daily events for Indian athletes from July 24 to August 2. Indian athletes aim to add to the nation's impressive Commonwealth Games medal history.

title: Stock Market LIVE Updates, Sensex Today: Sensex Drops 400 Points As Oil Touches $95 Amid US-Iran War
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty LIVE Updates: Crude oil prices climb to six-week high with Brent above $95 amid escalating US-Iran war.

title: Deleted Cockroach Post, Instagram 'Star': The Officer In CJP Firestorm
content: Sonia Sehrawat, an Assistant Commandant in the RAF which is a specialised wing of the Central Reserve Police Force (CRPF), made her thoughts about the student protests public in an Instagram story.

title: After CJP Protest Crackdown, Lawyers To Recite Preamble At Supreme Court Lawn
content: More than 650 lawyers on Wednesday issued a statement describing the police crackdown on the CJP-led student march to Parliament as an attack on constitutional values.

title: "Shameful If They Want To Poison Citizens": Supreme Court Slams Rajasthan Over Jojari River Pollution
content: The Supreme Court said that a recent report by an expert committee clearly indicates that "highly acidic" water is still flowing in the Jojari River.

title: Why Supreme Court Cancelled Sonam Raghuvanshi's Bail, Asked Her To Surrender
content: According to the top court, the allegations against Sonam are "quite serious", adding that she can apply for bail after six months if the trial is not completed.

title: Parliament Monsoon Session 2026 Live Updates: Centre Ready For NEET Debate Since Day One, Opposition Stalling House: Kiren Rijiju
content: Parliament Monsoon Session 2026 Live Updates: The proceedings in both Houses have been stalled because of government-opposition deadlock as the former insists on readiness for debate and the latter remains bent on the Education Minister's resignation before initiating debate.

title: Illegal Indian Arrested In US For 'Theft' Of Lethal Powder Worth $3-Million
content: Kumar's arrest records showed that he was caught hauling 10 pallets of stolen tungsten oxide powder, valued at $2,857,500.

title: Opinion: Opinion | CJP Protest And Opposition's Never-Ending Search For 'Readymade' Revolts
content: The Opposition cannot simply saunter in after anger has been created, and ask to become its beneficiary

title: Looking For Best Fixed Deposit Returns? Compare Top Bank Interest Rates
content: Experts advise investors to consider the financial strength of the bank, deposit tenure, liquidity needs and deposit insurance before investing.

title: India's Undernourished Population Falls 42%, But Cost Of Food Big Challenge: UN Report
content: Between 2019 and 2025, the number of Indians unable to afford a healthy diet also dropped by nearly 29 per cent.

title: Home Visits, WhatsApp Location: How Mumbai Cops Are Trying To Curb Protests
content: Mumbai police use digital tracking and home visits to prevent repeat participation in student protests.

title: CJP Protest LIVE Updates: 'Won't Spare Those Who Harm Future Of Our Youth,' Says PM Modi On Paper Leaks
content: CJP Jantar Mantar protest LIVE: The Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) protesters are continuing their sit-in at Jantar Mantar.

title: Centre vs Opposition Protest Showdown Outside Parliament Amid Stormy Session
content: The Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) supporters continued their sit-in at Jantar Mantar today, demanding accountability over alleged irregularities in competitive examinations, including the NEET-UG 2026.

title: US Judge Seeks Clarity On Hegseth's Testosterone Treatment Policy For Troops
content: The judge also requested information on the Pentagon's "basis for treating trans men and other service members differently between this new policy and the Military Ban."

title: Sundar Pichai Defends Google's AI Strategy Amid Gemini Delays
content: Investors are uneasy over Google delaying the release of Gemini 3.5 Pro. The call on Wednesday, Pichai struck an unusually defensive tone as analysts pressed him about Google's frontier models.

title: Meet Mumbai Woman Who Was Seen Blocking Police Van During CJP Protests
content: The woman was seen standing in front of a police van and refusing to move during student protests in Mumbai.

title: 9 Indian Fishermen Arrested By Sri Lankan Navy
content: The nine arrested fishermen and their seized trawler were handed over to the Fisheries Inspector of Mannar for further legal proceedings, the Navy said.

title: Hanuman Idol Stolen Near Tirumala Hills In Andhra Recovered Within 15 Hours
content: The incident raised concerns over security in Tirumala, with the opposition YSR Congress Party (YSRCP) questioning the security arrangements in and around the temple town.

title: 'Will Ask Shehbaz Sharif To Make Me PM': Nawaz Sharif's New PoK 'Desire'
content: Sharif claimed PoK was as important for him as his home province, Punjab. "It is my second home. My ancestors migrated from Kashmir."

title: Explained: Why Cotton is India's "White Gold"
content: a detailed backgrounder tracing cotton's journey from cultivation to export . it highlights government schemes aimed at improving productivity, quality and market access . cotton is called "white gold" because of its outsized economic importance in India .

title: CJP demands ADCP Lamba's suspension after viral slap video
content: the Cockroach Janata Party has demanded the suspension of Additional DCP Sandeep Lamba . the video purportedly shows him slapping a protester during the recent student demonstrations at Jantar Mantar . he is not mentally fit to serve in the pow .
